description_long: This object is a vintage, bazooka-style trumpet with a large, flared brass bell and a wooden or wood-textured stock at the opposite end. The body features a cylindrical metal tube with ribbed segments and a set of metallic, possibly decorative, elements along the side. A circular, disc-like stand or support is attached near the bell, suggesting it can be rested on a surface. The finish is a mix of aged brass, dark wood, and metallic greenish hues, giving it a rugged, utilitarian appearance. The instrument lacks visible traditional trumpet valves and instead has a unique, almost weapon-like design, reminiscent of a military or ceremonial horn.
description: A vintage, bazooka-style trumpet with a large brass bell, ribbed metal body, and wooden stock, featuring a disc-like support near the bell.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length, large flared bell, ribbed metal body, and wooden stock with a disc support visible near the bell.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide, flared brass bell and the alignment of the body and stock behind it.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same features as view 0 but from the other side, with the disc support and stock clearly visible.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the wooden stock and the cylindrical body, with the bell seen as a small circle at the far end.
attribute_bell_flare: The bell is wide and features a pronounced, abrupt flare, giving it a dramatic, open appearance.
attribute_valve_configuration: No visible valves are present; the body is smooth and uninterrupted by traditional valve mechanisms.
attribute_leadpipe_curvature: The leadpipe appears straight, running directly from the stock to the bell without significant curvature.
attribute_tuning_slide_location: No distinct main tuning slide is visible; the body is a continuous tube with no U-shaped or separate slide section.
